Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Nunc Pro Tunc Directing The Clerk To File, And To Correct Filing Date To May 17, 2018 On: (1) Petition For Writ Of Mandate To Correct, Modify Or Partially Vacate Arbitration Award And (2) Request For Judicial Notice And (3) Declaration Of Richard J. Idell In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ory Sandel 415-506-2400 of the Idell Firm for Defendant GSRV ET AL.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The Court informs Counsel it has relied on documents that have not been filed. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT GOLDEN STATE RENAISSANCE VENTURES, LLC, GSRV MANAGEMENT, LLC, GSRV I MANAGEMENT, LLC, GSRV II MANAGEMENT, LLC, GSRV III MANAGEMENT, LLC, SFBARC 5 MANAGEMENT, LLC, SFBARC 6 MANAGEMENT, LLC'S Motion For Order Nunc Pro Tunc Directing The Clerk To File, And To Correct Filing Date To May 17, 2018 On: (1) Petition For Writ Of Mandate To Correct, Modify Or Partially Vacate Arbitration Award And (2) Request For Judicial Notice And (3) Declaration Of Richard J. Idell In Support Thereof.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. GSRV Entities, SFBARC Entities and 3G Entities' motion for order nunc pro tunc directing the clerk to file certain papers as of May 17, 2018 is granted. Moving parties have shown good cause for the relief they seek. Consistent with the declaration of Glenda Cayabyab submitted in support of this motion, the court's records show that the sole reason why the papers presented for filing on May 17, 2018 and now sought to be filed nunc pro tunc as of that date were rejected for filing is that the clerk reviewing the papers believed that a court order was necessary for the papers to be filed because the papers included "3 new parties." While well-intentioned and perhaps in conformity with the instructions she received, the clerk should not have rejected the papers for the sole reason that she rejected them. No authority required the 3G Entities to obtain an order for them to join in the papers that were rejected.

In addition, the Court instructs Counsel to file the "internal minutes" of the Court and to serve them on the other side.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Application For Complete Stay Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Richard J. Idell of The Idell Firm (707.938.7763) appears as counsel for all defendants/moving parties except Steven Kay

No other appearance is made in this matter.

DEFENDANTS STEVEN KAY, GOLDEN STATE RENAISSANCE VENTURES, LLC, GSRV MANAGEMENT, LLC, GSRV I MANAGEMENT, LLC, GSRV II MANAGEMENT, LLC, GSRV III MANAGEMENT, LLC, SFBARC 5 MANAGEMENT, LLC AND SFBARC 6 MANAGEMENT, LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Application For Complete Stay Pending Arbitration is GRANTED and this case is stayed pending the completion of the arbitration proceedings. The operating agreements signed by Plaintiff and Defendants incorporate JAMS' commercial dispute rules and procedures. Per JAMS' rules, the parties gave the arbitrator the authority to decide preliminary issues of arbitrability. (Greenspan v. LADT, LLC (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 1413, 1442-1443; Rule 11 of JAMS' Comprehensive Arbitration Rules and Procedures). Under the doctrine of equitable estoppel, Plaintiff may be compelled to arbitrate despite her failure to sign operating agreements with Defendants SFBARC 5 Management, LLC and SFBARC 6 Management, LLC. Because Plaintiff seeks benefits under the SFBARC 5 and SFBARC 6 operating agreements , she is bound by the arbitration provisions in those agreements. (See Boucher v. Alliance Title Company (2005) 127 Cal. App, 4th 262, 269-70.) Written order is signed and filed in open court.
